questions.question: Explain with examples the three main types of ambiguity in natural language processing
expectedPoints: Lexical ambiguity: words with multiple meanings (e.g., 'bank')
Syntactic ambiguity: multiple parse trees for same sentence (e.g., prepositional phrase attachment)
Semantic ambiguity: sentences with multiple interpretations (e.g., 'The chicken is ready to eat')

questions.question: Discuss the major challenges in NLP and how they impact the development of language processing systems. Include specific examples and potential solutions
expectedPoints: Ambiguity at multiple levels (lexical, syntactic, semantic)
Context dependence and world knowledge requirements
Morphological complexity especially in Indian languages
Resource scarcity for less commonly taught languages
Cultural and pragmatic aspects of language
Approaches include statistical models, neural networks, hybrid systems, and knowledge representation
